title: Our Friends, the Robots
passage:
Our Friends, the Robots

Robots are smart machines. Today, we have many robots in our homes, schools, and offices. They help humans with many easy and hard jobs.

In the kitchen, some robots can cook simple food. For example, they can make soup or toast bread. Other robots can clean the floor. These cleaning robots are small and round. They move around the living room and clean dust. They are very quiet, so people can sleep or read books.

In schools, teachers use small robots to help children. These robots can talk, tell stories, and sing songs. They help children learn English and math. Children like these robots because they are friendly and have bright colors. Some schools also have robot clubs where students build their own robots.

In big factories, there are very large robots. These robots are strong and fast. They can lift heavy boxes and put them on big trucks. They do not get tired, and they can work all day and all night. This helps companies make things quickly.

In hospitals, robots help doctors and nurses. They can carry medicine and food to patients. Some robots can even clean the hospital rooms to keep them safe.

In the future, we will have more robots. Some robots will drive cars and buses. Other robots will help old people at home. Robots make our lives easier and better every day.

However, robots cannot do everything. They cannot feel happy or sad. They do not have feelings like humans. They also need electricity to work. If there is no electricity, they will stop working. But scientists are working hard to make better robots every year.

vocabulary.meaning: a large building where machines are used to make things
pills or liquid used to help sick people get better
power that flows through wires to make machines work

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Tìm từ khóa trực tiếp: Vì đây là trình độ A1-A2, từ khóa trong câu hỏi sẽ giống hệt từ khóa trong bài đọc. Hãy tìm các từ như 'kitchen', 'schools', 'factories', 'electricity' để xác định đúng đoạn văn chứa đáp án.

2. Lưu ý giới hạn từ: Với dạng Sentence Completion, luôn chú ý yêu cầu 'NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS'. Hãy điền chính xác từ trong bài đọc mà không thay đổi dạng từ.

3. Phân biệt FALSE và NOT GIVEN: Nếu bài đọc nói ngược lại thông tin trong câu hỏi, đáp án là FALSE. Nếu thông tin hoàn toàn không được nhắc tới (ví dụ giá tiền của robot), đáp án là NOT GIVEN.
